NXT-64484.summary: QA > Regression > IM > While changiing contributions in the menu item system is displaying 500 Error
NXT-64484.desc:
h2. Impact

Users are experiencing a 500 Error when attempting to change contributions in the Item management module. This prevents them from successfully updating menu items.

h2. Expected behaviour

The system should allow users to change contributions without displaying any errors.

h2. Actual behaviour

When changing contributions in the menu item, the system displays a 500 Error with the message:  
*Something is broken. Please post to the developer forums with additional details of your request, in case others are having similar issues.*

h2. Steps to reproduce

* Navigate to the Item management module.
* Click on the menu items tab.
* Select the menu item named Chicken SM [*Item LI-13289*].
* Attempt to change the contributions in MMA.

NXT-64595.summary: IM - Onboarding - Pack size & Sub Qty Multiple issues
NXT-64595.desc:
# We are not providing alert or highlights for any rows that have been skipped or worked through and not entered all the mandatory information
## This happens whether tabbing through a row or just completely skipping the row and moving onto the next item, the idea of this is to provide the user with a visual that they moved onto a row with a row above still not completed, there is nothing wrong with them doing that but we should highlight it so they are aware.
# A user marking an item as none/decide later should not be purged from the list until the user saves this information
## Dynamic behavior should only apply to the unit and sub qty fields
# When I click on hide toggle, the last unit on the last item I was working on gets cleared
# We are still not providing a Unsaved changes prompt when user is moving on to the update pack/serving link button and it is causing user to lose all information updated that has not been saved. (Scope changed to save the changes and redirect to pack/serving link screen)
# when switching from a number of inventory units with an item that has all the descriptions to none/decide later and then again clicking the # of inventory units, its like we are half way retaining the information but displaying it on different click events. Please see video to demonstrate. We either clear it all and let them start over or retain it all properly.

NXT-67009.summary: Regression - IM > Units Card - Standard volume measurement as an inventory-able serving/dual unit for a Non-fluid item.
NXT-67009.desc:
Standard volume measurement as an inventory-able serving/dual unit for a Non-fluid item. 

Standard volume measurement - A tablespoon cannot be directly made as an inventory-able unit -for non-fluid items-. We cannot add or edit the sub-quantity column for the tablespoon description. When editing, it functions as a label rather than an editable column.

However, this can be achieved indirectly.

!2025-10-03.mp4|width=320,alt="2025-10-03.mp4"!

!2025-10-03 (4).mp4|width=320,alt="2025-10-03 (4).mp4"!

*Expected behavior:* 

*For all items:*

* Standard Measures can never be created as a pack size unit, promoted to a dual unit, or linked as an inventory-able serving.
** If user tries to enter a standard measure for a pack size unit, provide an error on the Description field with an icon. Tooltip: Standard Measures cannot be created as pack size units.



*For Non-Fluid Items:*
Recommended measures should include standard non-fluid units, IsSolid fluid units(Teaspoon, Tablespoon, Cup) and common units. We are prefilling the weights for only standard non-fluid items based on the amount.

Conversions  should be between the IsSolid fluid units (Teaspoon, Tablespoon, Cup) only.

*For Fluid Items:*
Recommended measures should include standard fluid units and common units. We are prefilling the volumes for only standard non-fluid items based on the amount.

NXT-67563.summary: IM - Bulk Item - Overview Section Status Updates
NXT-67563.ac:
* Update overview to provide more statuses to give more visibility into truly required information
* Combine Item # and Description into a single column
** left-align alert icons 
** plan to do this on every sub-screen
* Change all edit pencils to a view icon
** move the view icon below the column heading, next to Item Count 
** Use a button instead of icon for the Item column
* Condense columns as small as possible
** Make some label changes to support this
*** Serving Info → Servings
*** Pack Size Info → Inventory Info
* Add an Images & Docs column, at the end of the grid
** show numeric chips to indicate how many images/docs are uploaded for that item
** clicking the chip will open the Images & Documents slide-out for that item
* Update the icon system
** refer the Section Status legend
** Disabled - 20% Black icon on 5% Black background
*** this is used for sections which are completely inapplicable to a particular item (ex: food columns for non-food item, or Menu Item column for non-Menu Item)
** Pending - Orange question mark icon
*** this is used for any section which is in scope of the session but is not yet populated

NXT-68467.summary: Item onboarding - pack size filters - PO observations
NXT-68467.desc:
Firstly, obviously there should be a way to deselect from the dropdown without using the Clear button. This is simply a single select dropdown; there should still be the X to clear the selection.

More importantly, I don’t know how we’re defining several of these flags.

This is a new session I have just started populating. In the video below I have not made any changes to the Pack Size section.

!20251117-2217-16.2525841.mp4|width=547,alt="20251117-2217-16.2525841.mp4"!

This would obviously mean several things:

* 6 out of 7 items are missing Pack-Serving Link. All items except the Non-Food item (Bleach) are missing this relationship. Yet when I select this option, 3 items are filtered. I’m not sure what we consider this link to be, but it’s not correct.
* Base Unit Weight should show only the items that require a weight but cannot be linked via Sub-Qty

!image-20251117-223312.png|width=531,alt="image-20251117-223312.png"!

In other words, BU Weight is a *subset* of Pack-Serving Link. Pack-Serving Link is ALL items missing the pack-serving relationship. BU Weight is the items that can ONLY be provided by Weight. If we look at the Serving Info, we can see several of these items are eligible to be linked via sub-qty (1 Each, 1 Tortilla, 1 Bowl). The other items (1/2 Cup, 1 Ounce) MUST be linked via weight. These are the items we’re expecting to see when we use this filter.

!image-20251117-223327.png|width=531,alt="image-20251117-223327.png"!

Additionally, this entire filter card section needs to be properly formatted, styled and sized. It’s not the right size, has no visible borders, etc. These issues are present on other sections as well. Please compare to Ingredient Info for comparison to proper styling, etc.

!image-20251117-223938.png|width=531,alt="image-20251117-223938.png"!

NXT-69523.summary: IM - Bulk Updates - Inventory Units & Sub-Qty
NXT-69523.ac:
Business Rule Updates

* Consume all inventory statuses for items added to Bulk Update sessions
** including Synced and Locked
* Update the Units & Sub-Qty sub-step to lock down relevant selections based on the inventory status
** the radio option cannot be modified for Locked and Synced items 
** Sub-Qty cannot be modified for Locked items
** Descriptions can always be modified, except for dual units (must be edited on Servings)
* Consume alert for duplicate pack size descriptions
** require user to provide unique description
* Consume dual units on this screen
** Display as label-only in Base Unit column
** Show ‘Dual’ chip next to Dual Unit servings

UX Updates

* Use single Item column with *(LI-1234) Item Name* formatting used on other sections
* Add Inventory status column at end of grid
NXT-69523.desc:
As an IM user I want the ability to bulk update unit descriptions and sub-quantities for items with various inventory statuses.

NXT-69524.ac:
Business Rule Updates

* Consume all inventory statuses for items added to Bulk Update sessions including Synced and Locked
* Update the Pack-Serving Link sub-step to lock down relevant selections based on the inventory status
* Sub-Quantities
** Provide serving size edit and selection for items with multiple eligible inventory-able servings 
*** ex: 1 Pizza, 1 Slice
*** default to largest eligible serving 
*** only for items not already synced/locked
** ‘Use Serving as Base Unit’ cannot be enabled for Locked (or Synced?) items
** ‘Provide Servings per Base Unit’ can always be enabled if the serving is countable, even for Locked/Synced items
* Pack-Serving Link - Weights
** Base Unit Weight can always be edited, but cannot be removed if it breaks pack-serving link (weight mandatory)
** Whole/Decimal option
*** Locked & Decimals cannot be modified (this is permanent for now) - disable the selection
*** Locked & Whole can be changed to Decimals 

UX Updates

* Use single Item column with *(LI-1234) Item Name* formatting used on other sections
* Add Inventory status column at end of grid

NXT-70100.summary: QA > Bulk update > Nutrients Bulk Review > Multiple observations
NXT-70100.desc:
*ISSUE - 1*

When clicking on a missing check box on Cholesterol, Sodium, we are adding Missing labels to both fat and carbs? They are not related to those at all.

!image-20260209-182911-20260210-060158.png|width=669,alt="image-20260209-182911-20260210-060158.png"!

!image-20260209-183046-20260210-060201.png|width=669,alt="image-20260209-183046-20260210-060201.png"!



However, If move the missing check box to trans fat, then the label is corrected for the Fat and is still appearing on the carbs.

*ISSUE - 2*

Notice on the above screen shot, how we have the (M) in bold, we should be doing this when the user clicks the box as well and also be consistent with all labeling.

!image-20260209-183203-20260210-060255.png|width=669,alt="image-20260209-183203-20260210-060255.png"!

!image-20260209-183141-20260210-060252.png|width=669,alt="image-20260209-183141-20260210-060252.png"!



*ISSUE - 3* 

Giving '0' count to the total fat automatically the counts will add in the *Sat. Fat (g)** & *Trans Fat (g)* 

*ISSUE - 4*


When a serving size picked on an item that has multiple serving sizes, why are we not adding –- for the columns like we are doing for items with one serving size. We should not be treating them any different as long as a serving size is selected. We are actually doing this for even items without a serving size selected so I am not sure what the logic is here.

!image-20260206-201352.png|width=941,alt="image-20260206-201352.png"!

 

*ISSUE - 5*

When selecting only Cholesterol for example as missing and nothing else, why are we all of a sudden flagging other values as missing? Also why are we using a orange color for missing instead of the red especially since these are mandatory values. Compare the color on the bean and cheese burrito vs the Buffalo Ranch Chicken Sticks for this example. Also once any value has been added for a particular item, we should be flagging the item as now missing mandatory/non mandatory information.

!image-20260206-202209.png|width=941,alt="image-20260206-202209.png"!

 

*ISSUE - 6*

We need to have proper spacing between the commas in the Fat and Carbs columns with the new combined values. We do not seem to be doing this correctly.

NXT-70658.desc:
* *Migration Testing Ownership* – Migration testing must be completed at least two days before production deployment. A designated QA resource should own this process and communicate any issues or missed deployments proactively during scrum meetings.
* *QA Thorough Check* – QA should pay extra attention before moving stories to PO review to avoid back‑and‑forth.
* *Improve Test Coverage* – Increase test coverage to ensure critical paths and new changes are thoroughly validated.
* *Early API Contract Resolution* – Resolve API contract–related stories within the first 2 days of the sprint.
* *Careful PR Review* – Dev should review PRs carefully before approving to ensure no existing functionality is broken.
* *Strengthen Code Reviews* – Ensure code reviews check for quality, standards, potential bugs, and impact before approval.
* *Reduce Bugs Through Better Code Quality* – Prevent stories from entering testing with multiple bugs; validate thoroughly before marking as complete.
* *Accurate Estimates* – Ensure estimates are solid before sprint planning; ask peers if unsure.
* *Demo Readiness* – Sync with UI/API/QA and verify against QA test cases before demos.
* *Better Collaboration on Stories* – After clarifying details with POs, add updates in the story’s comment section to keep everyone aligned.
* *Early Clarification* – Clarify early with POs or Order Managers to avoid back‑and‑forth between PO review and testing.

NXT-71186.summary: IM - Bulk Update - Pack Serving Link - Pack size overview should not merge the units when we toggle off the "Use serving as base unit" option. (PO Observation) - Regression
NXT-71186.desc:
* On the Sub-Quantities step, I changed the Dual Unit option to ‘No’
* I moved to the Weights step then switched back

!image-20260317-053931.png|width=671,alt="image-20260317-053931.png"!

* Now when I click the Dual Unit toggle to Yes, it _overwrites_ the Case instead of adding back the Carton. The Case is a distinct unit with its own weight. We should have some logic to prevent this from happening.
** I understand this logic somewhat conflicts with the logic we use on Item Onboarding. I think the issue is that we are *changing the # of Inventory Units* value. We cannot change that value based on this toggle. We are effectively changing that value from 2 to 1 when the user says they don’t want to use the Serving as a BU.
** Remember we are _merging_ units, not promoting the serving to a pack size. If the user switches this toggle, we should keep 2 units until they go to the Units & Sub-Qty step and change that value. It could be a placeholder, which would affect the inventory status. But the only step that should be altering the number of units is the step dedicated for that (Units & Sub-Qty)

→ Pack size overview should not merge the units when we toggle off the "Use serving as base unit" option. this is happening because of selecting standard unit cards with duel unit

NXT-71363.desc:
h3. *Code Quality & Reviews*

* *Careful PR Reviews* – Devs must review PRs thoroughly to ensure no existing functionality breaks.
* *Strengthen Code Reviews* – Check code quality, standards, potential bugs, and overall impact before approving.
* *Unit Testing Before QA* – Perform unit testing before sending stories to QA.
  Make sure to *tag QA, Sasi, and Raju* for visibility and tracking.

h3. *QA Process & Readiness*

* *Handling Stories With High Bug Count* – If any story has multiple bugs, and the defects are not resolved by EOD, move the story back to _In Progress_ from _Testing_ to ensure quality and proper re‑work.
* *QA Thorough Check* – QA should validate carefully before sending stories to PO review to reduce back‑and‑forth.
* *Demo Readiness* – Sync with UI/API/QA and verify against QA test cases before demos.
* *Migration Testing Ownership* – Migration testing should be completed at least *2 days before production deployment*.
A designated QA should own this and communicate issues or missed deployments in scrum meetings.

h3. *Estimation & Planning*

* *Accurate Estimates* – Ensure estimates are solid before sprint planning; ask peers if unsure.
* *Early Clarification* – Clarify stories early with POs or Dev Manager to avoid back‑and‑forth between PO review and testing.
* *Early API Contract Resolution* – Resolve API contract–related stories within the first *2 days* of the sprint.



h3. *Story Preparation & Delivery*

* *Better Collaboration on Stories* – After clarifying with POs, add updates in the story’s comments to keep everyone aligned.
* *Reduce Bugs Before QA* – Ensure stories do not carry multiple defects into QA; validate and test thoroughly before marking as ready.

h3. *QA Process & Readiness*

* *Handling Stories With High Bug Count* – If any story has multiple bugs, and the defects are not resolved by EOD, move the story back to _In Progress_ from _Testing_ to ensure quality and proper re‑work.
* *QA Thorough Check* – QA should validate carefully before sending stories to PO review to reduce back‑and‑forth.
